item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
An artisan was selling some little plush creatures on her Etsy store. She took photos, and gave them a name, and wrote up a little backstory for them.

The original SCP-1926 was about those little plush creatures: it used the photos that the artisan had taken, and the name she had given them, and the backstory she had written. And it didn't credit her.

For the first few years of the Foundation project, we had a bad habit of grabbing weird pictures from wherever and basing SCPs on them without getting permission from the copyright owners. But that article wasn't just based on someone else's photo, it was based entirely on the story she'd written about them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
JS Licensing Staff post

After review, I cannot find any paper trail of permissions for the first or third images and so I have removed them until that can be ascertained.

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded: Apologies for the necropost but what was plagiarized in the original 1926 again?

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded: sorry, what's the basis for us being allowed to use the original Atari work-for-hire promo art?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Can you give link on this post, please?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This one doesn't really grab me. Narrative-wise it's decent, but the "high-as-donkey-balls programmers trying to save a dying console company" topic doesn't go up my alley.

Tone/formatting/grammar/other issues
Close

Person of Interest #1377 is currently housed within Site-107

however, in the interview, he's referred to as PoI-1137

DNA testing of the tissue located within SCP-1926 has not been fruitful to date

"fruitful" doesn't sound clinical, you could use "successful", or "DNA testing of the tissue has been inconclusive"

and an cable of unknown purpose or design surgically attached to SCP-1926-A and SCP-1926-B's spines

besides the "an cable" issue, "spines" should be replaced with "vertebral columns"

PoI-1137 was arrested after police attempted to issue an in-person fine. Subsequently, law enforcement entered the home and discovered PoI-1137

This sounds like the police arrested him, then entered his home and found him there again

Having the last 2 interviews in collapsibles is good, but having the first one (which is also longer than the other 2) as-is isn't consistent, I'd put that too in a collapsible

And then there's the interviews' content, which really seems like they're trying too hard, have excessive amounts of colloquialisms, and are pretty incoherent overall.

It's nasty, covered to the BRIM with teeth and hair and shit… no, not like, poopy

is pretty unnecessary and doesn't add anything. There's more phrases like this one tho

But apparently the powers that be thought that was too grandiose

I can't figure out what this means

Close

The incident report has potential, but it doesn't make up for everything else. Overall -1, but if this sees some work I'll reconsider

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: just found a post with all the info i needed, if anyone is seeing this, i no longer need help.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: slightly confused as to what "plagiarism" means, what was being copied? i cant find any info on this but here, if anyone could give me more info on this it'd be nice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: you had me at Swordquest Airworld, then you lost me. this all just seems like weirdness for the sake of weirdness, with no attempt to tell a coherent story beyond "Nolan Bushnell forced people to make a thing."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
These are my thoughts (hopefully they mean something):

spoilers?
warned you.

The Conprocs and Description did not impress me. The only notable thing that made me have any sort of reaction was the plug in the spine (which, by the way. gross.) It might've just been me but how it was laid out and presented didnt really make sense for me. I actually really enjoy the Arcadia GoI so far as it stands, but how its described felt… unnecessary? The extra information also confused me a bit, but really that might be me.

In terms of narrative, eh? The base concept for it just feels a bit tropey. "Whoa look, these guys are stuck in a VR game whoaaa!" was the only thing I could really think of while reading. Nolan as a character seemed interesting, but it was a little overshadowed by the PoI's unnatural dialogue. Like, I understand what hes supposed to be, but his interactions/attitude felt really overdone.

For the listed reasons I have to give it a -1. Theres some other things, but I'm having trouble finding the words. The entire concept just feels weak, and the associated narrative doesnt really justify it enough for me.

EDIT: After looking at the final Addendum, I realize that it (talking about that final Addendum) does have potential. But, I just feel like it's not too widely explored. I actually almost forget it existed until I looked through the skip again. I really wish that was more explored, as that seems like an interesting enough twist for the story and concept.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Image Stuff
– hide block

The first image comes from here and Roget said we were given permission to use them

The second and third images was taken by Roget and used with permission

Fuck off, nobody loves you.

This is a collab with Roget

Originally this idea was formed a bit before Arcadia become a full blown GoI but it's being released now due to real life stuff that got in the way. Nonetheless, I hope you enjoy this.

Thanks to Brewsterion 1017, Popsioak, Tanhony, Deadly Bread and anyone else who looked at this before it went up.

Just in case anyone is confused: the original SCP-1926 was deleted due to plagiarism. This article has nothing to do with the original.
